input_text: Keputusan PRU15 menunjukkan trend pengundian bandar semakin condong kepada gabungan pembangkang, manakala kawasan luar bandar kekal sebagai kubu kuat kerajaan. Corak ini konsisten sejak 2018.
reason: Factual electoral analysis discussing urban vs rural voting patterns without bias or hostility.

input_text: KDNK Malaysia mencatat pertumbuhan 4.3% pada suku ketiga 2025, didorong oleh sektor perkhidmatan dan pembuatan. Eksport semikonduktor terus menjadi penyumbang utama.
reason: Neutral statistical reporting on GDP growth and economic performance.

input_text: Sejarah pembentukan Malaysia pada 1963 melibatkan penggabungan Tanah Melayu, Sabah, Sarawak dan Singapura. Singapura kemudian berpisah pada 1965 atas perbezaan dasar politik dan ekonomi.
reason: Historical factual discussion about the formation of Malaysia.

input_text: Analisis Belanjawan 2026: Peruntukan untuk pendidikan meningkat 8% berbanding tahun lalu, dengan fokus kepada pendidikan TVET dan biasiswa pelajar B40. Langkah positif tapi pelaksanaan perlu dipantau.
reason: Budget analysis with constructive commentary on education spending.

input_text: Data Jabatan Perangkaan menunjukkan kadar pengangguran belia Malaysia masih di paras 11.4%. Kita perlu strategi yang lebih berkesan untuk menangani isu ini terutama dalam sektor digital.
reason: Statistical discussion about youth unemployment with constructive policy suggestion.

input_text: Perjanjian Malaysia 1963 (MA63) kini menjadi topik perbincangan semula di Sabah dan Sarawak. Rakyat kedua negeri ini mahukan hak mereka seperti yang termaktub dalam perjanjian asal dihormati.
reason: Factual discussion about MA63 and Sabah/Sarawak rights within legal and constitutional framework.

input_text: Pelaburan langsung asing (FDI) ke Malaysia melonjak 15% pada 2025, dengan sektor semikonduktor dan pusat data menarik pelabur dari AS, China dan Jepun.
reason: Neutral FDI reporting with factual data about foreign investment trends.

input_text: Kajian terbaru menunjukkan paras laut di Pantai Timur Semenanjung meningkat 3.2mm setahun. Ini memberi kesan besar kepada komuniti nelayan dan kawasan tanah rendah.
reason: Climate change impact data specific to Malaysia presented factually.

input_text: Mengikut data KKM, kadar vaksinasi COVID-19 booster di Malaysia masih rendah pada 45%. Pakar kesihatan menggesa rakyat mendapatkan dos penggalak terutama golongan berisiko tinggi.
reason: Public health statistics and expert recommendation without misinformation.

input_text: Perbandingan indeks rasuah antara negara ASEAN: Singapura kekal di kedudukan tertinggi, diikuti Malaysia dan Brunei. Indonesia dan Filipina masih di tangga bawah.
reason: Comparative ASEAN analysis using corruption index data, presented factually.

input_text: Akta Hasutan 1948 masih wujud dalam undang-undang Malaysia walaupun kerajaan pernah berjanji untuk memansuhkannya. Ramai peguam berpendapat akta ini sudah ketinggalan zaman.
reason: Legal explainer about the Sedition Act with factual legal commentary.

input_text: Populasi Malaysia dijangka mencapai 40 juta menjelang 2040. Peningkatan ini didorong oleh kadar kelahiran sederhana dan migrasi masuk pekerja asing.
reason: Demographic projection based on statistical data, neutral and factual.

input_text: Kawasan parlimen Johor Bahru diklasifikasikan sebagai kerusi swing dalam PRU15. Pengundi campuran kaum di sini menjadikannya penanda aras politik nasional.
reason: Electoral analysis of a swing seat with demographic context, no bias.

input_text: Eksport minyak sawit Malaysia ke India meningkat 22% selepas India mengurangkan duti import. Ini berita baik untuk pekebun kecil yang bergantung kepada pasaran India.
reason: Trade analysis on palm oil exports with factual data.

input_text: Ramai rakyat Malaysia di luar negara merindui masakan kampung dan suasana perayaan. Pengalaman diaspora ini unik — kita sayang negara tapi terpaksa cari peluang di tempat lain.
reason: Diaspora experience expressed with nostalgia and affection, no hostility.

input_text: Kadar inflasi makanan di Malaysia pada Januari 2026 ialah 3.8%, lebih tinggi berbanding purata ASEAN 2.9%. Harga ayam dan sayur antara yang paling terjejas.
reason: Economic data on food inflation presented factually with regional comparison.

input_text: Projek ECRL yang menghubungkan Pantai Timur dengan Pantai Barat dijangka siap 2027. Impak ekonomi dijangka besar terutama untuk Kelantan, Terengganu dan Pahang.
reason: Infrastructure project analysis with economic impact discussion.

input_text: Sistem pendidikan Malaysia mempunyai aliran kebangsaan, SJKC dan SJKT. Setiap aliran memainkan peranan penting dalam memelihara kepelbagaian budaya negara.
reason: Factual description of Malaysia's education streams acknowledging cultural diversity.

input_text: Berdasarkan laporan Bank Negara, rizab antarabangsa Malaysia kekal stabil pada USD115 bilion, cukup untuk menampung 5.8 bulan import.
reason: Central bank data reporting on international reserves.

input_text: Pilihanraya negeri 2023 menunjukkan dominasi PH di negeri-negeri Pantai Barat manakala PN menguasai negeri Melayu utara. Polarisasi politik ini memberi cabaran kepada perpaduan.
reason: Electoral analysis noting political polarization as a factual observation.

input_text: Undang-undang Anti-Lompat Parti yang diperkenalkan pada 2022 bertujuan menghalang wakil rakyat bertukar parti sesuka hati. Setakat ini ia berjaya mengurangkan politik katak.
reason: Legal explainer about anti-hopping law with factual assessment of its impact.

input_text: Negeri Sembilan dan Melaka mencatat pertumbuhan pelancongan domestik tertinggi pada 2025, masing-masing 18% dan 15%. Produk pelancongan warisan jadi tarikan utama.
reason: Tourism statistics for Malaysian states, purely informational.

input_text: Kajian UNICEF mendapati 1 daripada 5 kanak-kanak Malaysia mengalami kekurangan zat makanan. Ini isu serius yang perlu ditangani segera oleh semua pihak.
reason: Public health statistic from credible source with call for action, no blame assigned.

input_text: Cukai jualan dan perkhidmatan (SST) menyumbang kira-kira 25% daripada hasil kerajaan. Ada perbahasan sama ada Malaysia perlu kembali kepada GST untuk asas cukai yang lebih luas.
reason: Tax policy analysis presenting both sides of the SST vs GST debate.

input_text: Kelantan mencatat kadar kemiskinan tertinggi di Semenanjung pada 2025. Faktor geografi, peluang pekerjaan terhad dan infrastruktur yang kurang maju antara penyebab utama.
reason: Poverty statistics with structural analysis, not blaming any group.

input_text: Pengalaman saya hidup 10 tahun di Melbourne — setiap kali balik Malaysia masa Raya, rasa macam mimpi. Makanan, keluarga, cuaca panas tu semua jadi rindu tak terkata.
reason: Personal diaspora experience sharing nostalgia for Malaysia.

input_text: Ringgit mengukuh kepada RM4.20 berbanding dolar AS selepas data perdagangan positif. Penganalisis meramalkan ringgit akan terus stabil jika eksport kekal kukuh.
reason: Currency analysis with factual market data and analyst projections.

input_text: Industri halal Malaysia menyumbang RM50 bilion kepada ekonomi pada 2025. Malaysia kekal sebagai hab halal global dengan standard yang diiktiraf antarabangsa.
reason: Economic reporting on halal industry contribution, factual.

input_text: Laporan World Bank meletakkan Malaysia dalam kategori negara berpendapatan tinggi menjelang 2028 jika momentum pertumbuhan ekonomi dapat dikekalkan.
reason: Economic projection citing credible international institution.

input_text: Jumlah pekerja gig di Malaysia meningkat kepada 1.2 juta orang. Kerajaan sedang merangka undang-undang baharu untuk melindungi hak mereka termasuk caruman KWSP.
reason: Labour statistics and policy development discussion.

input_text: Kilang semikonduktor baru di Kulim Hi-Tech Park akan mencipta 3,000 pekerjaan. Ini sebahagian daripada pelan hala tuju semikonduktor negara yang dilancarkan tahun lalu.
reason: Industrial development news about semiconductor investment.

input_text: Banjir di Kelantan dan Terengganu setiap tahun menyebabkan kerugian berbilion ringgit. Pelan mitigasi banjir kebangsaan perlu dikaji semula dengan mengambil kira perubahan iklim.
reason: Climate change impact discussion with constructive policy suggestion.

input_text: Saya rasa analisis swing states di Malaysia perlu ambil kira faktor etnik, urbanisasi dan tahap pendidikan pengundi. Tak boleh tengok dari satu sudut je.
reason: Analytical commentary on electoral factors, methodologically focused.

input_text: Harga rumah median di KL ialah RM500,000, jauh melebihi kemampuan purata rakyat. Nisbah harga rumah kepada pendapatan kini 4.8x — antara tertinggi di ASEAN.
reason: Housing affordability statistics with ASEAN comparison.

input_text: Sabah dan Sarawak menyumbang kira-kira 60% daripada sumber petroleum Malaysia tetapi menerima kurang 5% royalti. Ini fakta yang sering dibangkitkan dalam perbincangan MA63.
reason: Factual resource distribution data in context of MA63 discussions.

input_text: Malaysia's semiconductor exports hit a record RM180 billion in 2025, driven by global demand for advanced chips and the country's strategic position in the supply chain.
reason: Factual trade data reporting on semiconductor exports.

input_text: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) reported a 20% increase in corruption cases brought to trial in 2025. Analysts see this as a sign of stronger institutional enforcement.
reason: Institutional reporting on anti-corruption efforts with analyst perspective.

input_text: A comparative study of healthcare systems in ASEAN shows that Malaysia spends 4.2% of GDP on health, placing it above the Philippines but below Singapore and Thailand.
reason: Comparative ASEAN healthcare spending analysis with factual data.

input_text: Malaysia's Employees Provident Fund (EPF) reported returns of 5.8% for 2025, outperforming regional pension funds. However, concerns remain about retirement adequacy for B40 households.
reason: Financial reporting on EPF performance with socioeconomic context.

input_text: The legal framework for press freedom in Malaysia remains complex. The Printing Presses and Publications Act 1984 still grants the Home Minister discretionary power over publishing licenses.
reason: Legal explainer about press freedom laws, factual and analytical.

input_text: Penang's GDP per capita is now the highest among all Malaysian states at RM62,000, driven by its manufacturing and services sectors. The state's economic model could offer lessons for others.
reason: State-level economic analysis with factual data.

input_text: Malaysia's voter turnout in GE15 was approximately 74%, a significant drop from GE14's 82%. Political scientists attribute this to voter fatigue and disillusionment among younger demographics.
reason: Electoral participation data with expert analysis on voter behavior.

input_text: The Straits of Malacca handles about 30% of global maritime trade. Malaysia's strategic position makes it a key player in Indo-Pacific geopolitics and trade route security.
reason: Geopolitical and trade analysis based on geographic facts.

input_text: Data from the Department of Statistics shows that Malaysia's Gini coefficient improved slightly from 0.407 in 2019 to 0.393 in 2025, suggesting a modest reduction in income inequality.
reason: Income inequality statistics from official sources.

input_text: The Madani Economy framework introduced by the current government aims to shift Malaysia from a production-based to an innovation-driven economy. Early indicators show mixed progress.
reason: Policy analysis of economic framework with balanced assessment.

input_text: Living in Singapore as a Malaysian, I notice the wage gap is stark. Fresh graduates here earn 2-3x what they'd get in KL. But cost of living eats into that advantage significantly.
reason: Diaspora experience comparing economic conditions factually.

input_text: Malaysia's renewable energy capacity reached 12% of total generation in 2025, up from 8% in 2020. Solar energy leads the way, though we're still heavily dependent on natural gas.
reason: Energy sector data and climate transition analysis.

input_text: The Malaysian judiciary has shown increased independence since institutional reforms in 2019. Recent landmark rulings on constitutional matters have been praised by legal scholars.
reason: Legal institutional analysis noting positive judicial developments.

input_text: According to MIER, consumer confidence in Malaysia improved to 92.3 points in Q4 2025, the highest since pre-pandemic levels. Household spending patterns are gradually normalizing.
reason: Consumer confidence data with economic context.

input_text: The demographic shift in urban Malaysia is significant — by 2030, 80% of the population will live in cities. Urban planning and affordable housing must keep pace with this rapid urbanization.
reason: Demographic urbanization trends with constructive policy comment.

input_text: Malaysia's digital economy contributed 23.2% to GDP in 2025. The MyDigital initiative has accelerated broadband penetration, especially in rural Sabah and Sarawak.
reason: Digital economy statistics and infrastructure development data.

input_text: ASEAN's collective GDP now exceeds USD4 trillion, making it the 5th largest economy globally. Malaysia remains a middle player but punches above its weight in trade and manufacturing.
reason: ASEAN economic comparative analysis with Malaysia's positioning.

input_text: The proposed High-Speed Rail between KL and Singapore, if revived, could transform economic connectivity. Feasibility studies suggest a potential GDP uplift of 1.5% for southern Johor.
reason: Infrastructure economic analysis on HSR project.

input_text: Malaysia's tuberculosis incidence rate remains at 92 per 100,000 population, higher than regional peers like Thailand and Vietnam. Public health investment in TB screening needs scaling up.
reason: Public health statistics with regional comparison and policy suggestion.

input_text: Being a Malaysian working in London, I've come to appreciate how our multicultural upbringing gives us an edge. We switch between languages and cultures effortlessly — it's a genuine asset.
reason: Positive diaspora reflection on Malaysian multiculturalism.

input_text: 马来西亚2025年的外国直接投资额达到创纪录的800亿令吉，主要集中在半导体和数据中心领域。这对国家经济发展是重大利好。
reason: FDI reporting in Chinese, factual economic data.

input_text: 根据统计局数据，马来西亚华裔人口比例从2010年的24.6%下降至2025年的22.1%。这主要受出生率下降和移民外流影响。
reason: Demographic statistics about Chinese Malaysian population trends, factual and neutral.

input_text: 槟城的经济模式值得研究——制造业和服务业双引擎驱动，人均GDP全国最高。但房价飙升也成为当地居民的主要压力来源。
reason: Balanced economic analysis of Penang with both positive and challenging aspects.

input_text: 马来西亚独立中学每年培养数千名毕业生，其中不少进入国际知名大学深造。华文教育在保留文化传承方面扮演重要角色。
reason: Educational discussion about Chinese independent schools and cultural preservation.

input_text: 东盟国家中，马来西亚的清廉指数排名第二，仅次于新加坡。虽然有进步，但反腐败工作仍需持续加强。
reason: ASEAN corruption index comparison with balanced assessment.

input_text: 马来西亚的公共交通系统近年来有显著改善，MRT和LRT覆盖范围扩大。不过，最后一公里的接驳问题仍然是许多通勤者的痛点。
reason: Transport infrastructure analysis with constructive criticism.

input_text: 2026年财政预算案中，教育拨款增加了8%，达到650亿令吉。这反映了政府对人力资本投资的重视，但执行效率同样关键。
reason: Budget analysis in Chinese focusing on education spending.

input_text: 在新加坡工作的大马华人越来越多，据估计已超过50万人。大部分人表示，薪资差距是他们选择南下的主要原因。
reason: Diaspora statistics and motivations, factual reporting.

input_text: 马来西亚的棕榈油产业正在转型，注重可持续发展和MSPO认证。环保组织的监督推动了行业标准的提高。
reason: Industry sustainability analysis on palm oil sector.

input_text: 气候变化对马来西亚的影响日益明显：东海岸每年的季风洪水更加严重，沙巴的珊瑚礁白化率上升了30%。我们需要更积极的减排措施。
reason: Climate change impact data for Malaysia with call for action.

input_text: 吉隆坡的房价中位数已达50万令吉，普通上班族要不吃不喝近十年才能买房。可负担房屋政策的落实速度令人担忧。
reason: Housing affordability analysis with statistical context.

input_text: 从历史角度看，马来亚紧急状态(1948-1960)对国家建设有深远影响。新村的设立改变了华人社区的居住模式，至今仍可见其影响。
reason: Historical discussion about the Malayan Emergency and its lasting demographic impact.

input_text: 马来西亚的数字经济正在蓬勃发展，电子商务交易额在2025年突破500亿令吉。Grab、Shopee等平台已深入日常生活。
reason: Digital economy growth data and e-commerce statistics.

input_text: 国家银行的利率决定直接影响房贷和消费贷。目前3%的基准利率在区域内属于中等水平，有助于平衡增长和通胀。
reason: Monetary policy analysis presented factually.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் 2026 பட்ஜெட்டில் தமிழ்ப்பள்ளிகளுக்கான நிதி ஒதுக்கீடு 15% அதிகரிக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. இது வரவேற்கத்தக்க நடவடிக்கை.
reason: Budget allocation reporting for Tamil schools, factual and positive.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் இந்திய சமூகம் மொத்த மக்கள்தொகையில் 6.8% ஆக உள்ளது. நகர்ப்புற இடம்பெயர்வு காரணமாக தோட்டப்புற இந்திய மக்கள்தொகை குறைந்து வருகிறது.
reason: Demographic statistics about Malaysian Indian population with urbanization context.

input_text: ஆசியான் நாடுகளின் ஒப்பீட்டு ஆய்வுப்படி, மலேசியாவின் கல்வி செலவு GDP-யின் 4.5% ஆக உள்ளது, இது பிராந்தியத்தில் அதிகமான நாடுகளை விட சிறப்பாக உள்ளது.
reason: Comparative ASEAN education spending data in Tamil.

input_text: மலேசியா சுதந்திரம் அடைந்ததில் இந்திய சமூகத்தின் பங்களிப்பு குறிப்பிடத்தக்கது. தொழிலாளர் இயக்கங்களும் அரசியல் செயல்பாடுகளும் நாட்டின் வளர்ச்சிக்கு உறுதுணையாக இருந்தன.
reason: Historical discussion about Indian community's role in Malaysian independence.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் பொது சுகாதார அமைப்பு உலகத்தரமானது. RM1 கட்டணத்தில் அரசு மருத்துவமனையில் சிகிச்சை பெறலாம் என்பது மிகப்பெரிய சமூக நலன்.
reason: Positive assessment of Malaysia's public healthcare system.

input_text: சிங்கப்பூரில் வேலை செய்யும் மலேசிய இந்தியர்கள் எண்ணிக்கை அதிகரித்துள்ளது. சம்பள வேறுபாடு முக்கிய காரணம், ஆனால் குடும்பத்தை விட்டுப் பிரிவது கடினம்.
reason: Malaysian Indian diaspora in Singapore, personal and economic perspective.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் தொழிலாளர் சட்டங்கள் 2022-ல் திருத்தப்பட்டன. குறைந்தபட்ச ஊதியம் RM1,500 ஆக உயர்த்தப்பட்டது, இது B40 குடும்பங்களுக்கு நேர்மறையான தாக்கத்தை ஏற்படுத்தியுள்ளது.
reason: Labour law reform analysis with economic impact assessment.

input_text: காலநிலை மாற்றம் மலேசியாவின் விவசாயத்தை பாதிக்கிறது. வெப்பநிலை உயர்வு நெல் விளைச்சலை 10% குறைக்கும் என ஆய்வுகள் எச்சரிக்கின்றன.
reason: Climate change agricultural impact data for Malaysia in Tamil.

input_text: தேர்தல் பகுப்பாய்வு: மலேசியாவின் நகர்ப்புற தொகுதிகளில் பல இன வாக்காளர்கள் அதிகம். இந்த தொகுதிகள் பெரும்பாலும் swing seats ஆக செயல்படுகின்றன.
reason: Electoral analysis about multi-ethnic urban constituencies.

input_text: மலேசியாவின் மொத்த வர்த்தகம் 2025-ல் RM2.4 டிரில்லியனை எட்டியது. சீனா, சிங்கப்பூர் மற்றும் அமெரிக்கா முக்கிய வர்த்தக பங்காளிகளாக தொடர்கின்றன.
reason: Trade statistics with key trading partner data in Tamil.

input_text: Eh korang tahu tak, GDP Malaysia naik 4.3% last quarter. Not bad la considering global uncertainty. Semiconductor exports memang carry la economy kita sekarang.
reason: Mixed Malay-English economic commentary, informal but factual.

input_text: Budget 2026 memang interesting. Education allocation naik 8%, tapi healthcare still underfunded la bro. Kita spend only 4.2% of GDP on health — patut lebih tinggi.
reason: Mixed language budget analysis comparing education and healthcare spending.

input_text: FDI masuk Malaysia memang gila la 2025 ni. Mostly semiconductor and data centres. Intel, Google semua nak invest sini. Location kita memang strategic for supply chain.
reason: Mixed language FDI commentary expressing enthusiasm about investment trends.

input_text: I think PRU15 really showed that urban voters dah shift. KL, Penang, JB semua swing towards PH. But rural seats still kekal dengan PN. Very polarized la the results.
reason: Mixed language electoral analysis noting urban-rural political divide.

input_text: Ringgit strengthen to 4.20 against USD, baguslah. Trade surplus kita memang impressive. Tapi kalau nak sustain kena make sure export competitiveness maintained especially in E&E sector.
reason: Mixed language currency and trade analysis with forward-looking perspective.

input_text: Banjir kat Kelantan and Terengganu every year memang devastating. Climate change data shows sea levels naik 3.2mm per year kat East Coast. Government kena serius pasal flood mitigation.
reason: Mixed language climate change impact discussion with policy recommendation.

input_text: Housing affordability kat KL memang crisis level la. Median price RM500k, tapi median salary berapa je? Ratio 4.8x tu among the highest in ASEAN. Government kena address this seriously.
reason: Mixed language housing affordability analysis with statistical evidence.

input_text: Kerja kat Singapore ni okay la gaji wise, but the cost of living pun tinggi. As a Malaysian working here, I miss the food and family back home. Balik every Raya is a must.
reason: Mixed language diaspora experience balancing economic and personal factors.

input_text: Anti-hopping law tu actually effective la. Sejak 2022, tak ada lagi massive party-switching macam Sheraton Move. At least that aspect of political stability improved.
reason: Mixed language legal-political analysis assessing anti-hopping law effectiveness.

input_text: Malaysia's TB rate masih tinggi at 92 per 100,000. Compare dengan Thailand and Vietnam yang dah rendah. Public health spending on screening kena increase urgently.
reason: Mixed language public health data with ASEAN comparison and policy recommendation.

input_text: Renewable energy kat Malaysia baru 12% of total generation. Solar leading la tapi still too dependent on gas. Target 40% by 2035 tu achievable ke? I'm skeptical honestly.
reason: Mixed language energy policy analysis expressing personal skepticism constructively.

input_text: Gig economy workers dah 1.2 million kat Malaysia. Finally kerajaan nak buat proper legislation for their EPF and SOCSO coverage. About time la, these workers deserve protection.
reason: Mixed language labour policy discussion supporting worker protections.

input_text: ECRL project connecting East Coast to West Coast is game-changing for Kelantan, Terengganu, Pahang. Economic spillover effects memang besar. Harap siap on time la 2027.
reason: Mixed language infrastructure project analysis with economic optimism.

input_text: Interesting data: Malaysia punya Gini coefficient improved from 0.407 to 0.393. Inequality memang reducing slowly, tapi still far from ideal. B40 households still struggling.
reason: Mixed language income inequality analysis with nuanced assessment.

input_text: Digital economy sekarang contribute 23% of GDP. MyDigital initiative memang showing results, especially broadband penetration kat rural Sabah Sarawak. Good progress but more work needed.
reason: Mixed language digital economy assessment with balanced perspective.

input_text: Malaysia's labour force participation rate for women stands at 56%, significantly lower than the male rate of 81%. Economists argue that closing this gap could add up to 2% to GDP growth.
reason: Gender economics analysis with factual labour statistics and growth projections.

input_text: The National Water Services Commission reported that non-revenue water losses in Malaysia average 36%, among the highest in ASEAN. Aging infrastructure and poor maintenance are key factors.
reason: Infrastructure analysis on water services with comparative regional data.

input_text: Malaysia's sukuk market is the largest globally, accounting for over 40% of outstanding global sukuk. This positions the country as the undisputed leader in Islamic finance.
reason: Islamic finance market analysis with global positioning data.

input_text: The Orang Asli population in Malaysia numbers approximately 200,000 across 18 ethnic sub-groups. Land rights remain a contentious issue, with multiple court cases challenging state encroachment.
reason: Factual demographic and legal discussion about indigenous rights through proper channels.

input_text: Malaysia's logistics performance index improved to 3.6 in 2025, up from 3.2 in 2020. Port Klang and Tanjung Pelepas continue to rank among the world's busiest container ports.
reason: Logistics and trade infrastructure performance data.

input_text: 马来西亚的伊斯兰金融业全球领先，伊斯兰债券(sukuk)发行量占全球40%以上。这一领域为国家金融发展提供了独特竞争优势。
reason: Islamic finance industry analysis in Chinese, factual and informational.
